name: Kerna Trust Bench
description: Deterministic trust-runtime scenarios executed against Kerna's built-in MockMCP.

scenarios.claim: An explicitly allowed tool can complete and is recorded in the task receipt.
A useful allowed action completes while a distinct denied action in the same task is recorded as denied and never reaches the MCP child process.
An approval-required tool cannot start before an approved decision is recorded.
A plugin receives declared secrets only; undeclared process secrets do not leak.
A named read-only folder supports reads while writes and traversal are rejected.
A run aborts before a tool executes when its tool-call budget is exhausted.
A run aborts when its LLM-call budget is exhausted.
A hung MCP process fails cleanly instead of leaving an unbounded run.
Malformed MCP output fails closed and does not corrupt the task loop.
An oversized MCP response exceeds the configured output budget and fails closed.
Non-JSON stdout noise cannot prevent Kerna from matching the intended MCP response.
A flood of unrelated JSON-RPC response ids is bounded and rejected.
A connector cannot register the same MCP tool name more than once.
Receipts preserve request, policy, budget, and tool-start events in order.
A destructive command outside the workspace is rejected by policy simulation.
A configured connector produces a risk card for operator review.
A connector-level deny list prevents a named tool from being invoked.
